rationale_for_baseline_tier: A dependent, frail older adult with unexplained bilateral injuries inconsistent with the reported mechanism, an active hostile caregiver posing immediate physical danger, objective neglect (Stage II pressure injury, malnutrition, dehydration) and possible occult serious trauma that cannot be excluded remotely. This is an immediate threat to life and safety: emergency ambulance transfer to ED (000) with discreet safeguarding activation is the only appropriate response. The escalation must use a neutral medical justification (urgent imaging and IV rehydration) so as not to alert or provoke the caregiver.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any sign of occult serious injury — new/worsening confusion or reduced consciousness, severe chest or abdominal pain, breathing difficulty, or a new deformity/inability to move a limb
escalation_edges.rationale: Occult intracranial, thoracic, abdominal injury or fracture can decompensate rapidly and cannot be excluded remotely.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she becomes drowsy or confused, has bad chest or tummy pain, struggles to breathe, or can't move a limb, that is an emergency — keep 000 on the line and tell them straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Immediate physical danger from the caregiver, or the patient at risk of being prevented from leaving safely
escalation_edges.rationale: An active perpetrator is present; ensuring physical safety and a safe extraction via emergency services takes priority, without confronting the caregiver.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Use a calm medical reason for the ambulance — she needs an urgent scan and a drip for dehydration — and let the paramedics and hospital manage safety. If anyone is in immediate danger, call 000 and say so.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Signs of worsening dehydration/shock — no urine output, marked drowsiness, cold clammy skin, or fainting
escalation_edges.rationale: Dehydration with AKI/haemodynamic instability in a frail elder off her antihypertensive can progress to shock.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she stops passing urine, becomes very drowsy, goes cold and clammy, or faints, treat it as an emergency and tell 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: The accompanying caregiver is controlling and hostile and is the source of the suspected harm
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Do not confront or tip off the caregiver; use a neutral medical justification for transfer and activate safeguarding discreetly. Attempt a private word with the patient only if it can be done safely.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Frail, bedbound, fully dependent older adult who under-reports and minimises
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Maintain a low threshold for escalation; atypical presentation and under-reporting mean occult injury and deterioration are easily missed.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Dependent on the alleged perpetrator, no water/call bell in reach, lapsed medication amid financial deterioration
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: The patient cannot self-protect or self-manage; safety depends on emergency services and safeguarding, not on any home-based plan.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This needs emergency hospital assessment tonight. Call 000 for an ambulance and explain she needs an urgent scan of her injuries and a drip because she is dehydrated. Keep the reason calm and medical. Stay with her, keep her comfortable and offered small sips of water if she can drink, and let the paramedics take over her care and safety. Do not give any strong pain medicine at home.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call 000 for an emergency ambulance now
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 is available 24 hours — this cannot wait for clinic hours. A discreet safeguarding/elder-abuse referral is made in parallel.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document the injury pattern, neglect findings, hydration concern, the caregiver interaction and the safeguarding referral for the ED team, hospital social work and the GP. The hospital will lead mandatory reporting and a safe discharge plan.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Accepting the fall story and treating as a benign mechanical fall / home analgesia, missing the safeguarding emergency and possible occult trauma.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The patient is left with an active abuser and unexamined occult injury — risk of catastrophic harm or death.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routing to routine urgent in-person review without emergency transfer or safeguarding activation understates the immediate danger.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Over-triage is not a realistic risk in this case; the presentation genuinely warrants emergency escalation.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Maltreatment cannot be forensically confirmed remotely, and occult serious injury cannot be excluded on a telehealth call.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the high-suspicion safeguarding picture and possible occult trauma as an emergency: escalate to ED/000 and activate safeguarding, rather than seeking to confirm remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Waiting for certainty, accepting the innocuous history, prescribing to the caregiver's demand, or attempting home management.
